Name: Thermal Protection System
Description: The spacecraft shall maintain internal temperatures between -20°C and +50°C during all mission phases, including launch, orbital transfer, and station-keeping.

Name: Structural Load Tolerance
Description: The primary structure shall withstand quasi-static loads of up to 8.5g axial and 3.0g lateral during launch without permanent deformation.

Name: Vibration Isolation Requirements
Description: The payload adapter shall attenuate random vibration levels to below 0.04 g²/Hz at the payload interface across 20–2000 Hz band.

Name: Avionics Power Budget
Description: The avionics subsystem shall operate within a total power budget of 450W during nominal operations, with a 15% margin for contingency modes.

Name: Solar Array Sizing
Description: The solar array shall generate a minimum of 2.8 kW end-of-life (EOL) power in worst-case sun angle and degradation conditions.

Name: Battery Capacity and Cycling
Description: The battery system shall provide a minimum of 1.2 kWh usable capacity with a cycle life exceeding 30,000 charge/discharge cycles at 40% depth of discharge.

Name: RF Communication Link Budget
Description: The S-band communication system shall close the link with a minimum 3 dB margin at a data rate of 2 Mbps from GEO to a 7.3m ground antenna.

Name: Antenna Pointing Accuracy
Description: The high-gain antenna shall maintain pointing accuracy within ±0.15° (3σ) of the commanded boresight direction during normal operations.

Name: Orbit Determination Accuracy
Description: The onboard navigation system shall determine the spacecraft position to within 50m (1σ) in each axis using GPS L1/L5 signals.

Name: Attitude Control Precision
Description: The ADCS shall maintain nadir pointing accuracy within ±0.05° (3σ) in roll, pitch, and yaw under nominal disturbance torques.

Name: Reaction Wheel Momentum Management
Description: The momentum management system shall desaturate reaction wheels using magnetic torquers, maintaining wheel speeds below 4000 RPM during nominal operations.

Name: MLI Blanket Coverage
Description: Multi-layer insulation blankets shall cover a minimum of 85% of the spacecraft external surface area, with a maximum effective emittance of 0.02.

Name: Heater Control System
Description: The thermal control system shall provide independently controllable heater circuits for each critical component, with autonomous thermostat control and ground-commandable override.

Name: Propulsion System Delta-V
Description: The propulsion system shall provide a minimum delta-V of 320 m/s for orbit raising, station-keeping, and end-of-life disposal maneuvers.

Name: Thruster Specific Impulse
Description: The monopropellant thrusters shall deliver a minimum specific impulse of 220 seconds at beginning of life.

Name: Electromagnetic Compatibility
Description: All electronic subsystems shall comply with MIL-STD-461G RE102 and CE102 emission limits and RS103 and CS101 susceptibility requirements.

Name: Radiation Hardness Assurance
Description: All electronic components shall withstand a total ionizing dose (TID) of 50 krad(Si) with a radiation design margin of 2x over the predicted mission dose.

Name: Ground Station Interface
Description: The ground segment interface shall comply with CCSDS 131.0-B-4 for telecommand encoding and CCSDS 132.0-B-3 for telemetry frame formatting.

Name: Software Fault Tolerance
Description: The flight software shall implement triple modular redundancy (TMR) for all safety-critical functions and autonomous fault detection, isolation, and recovery (FDIR) within 500ms of fault onset.

Name: Telemetry Data Rate
Description: The housekeeping telemetry system shall transmit a minimum of 200 health-and-status parameters at a rate of 1 Hz during nominal operations and 10 Hz during anomaly investigation.

Name: Mass Budget Allocation
Description: The total spacecraft wet mass shall not exceed 1,850 kg, with a minimum 10% mass margin maintained at the system level through CDR.

Name: Debris Mitigation Compliance
Description: The spacecraft shall comply with NASA-STD-8719.14 orbital debris mitigation requirements, including a 25-year post-mission disposal timeline and a casualty risk below 1:10,000.

Name: Launch Vehicle Compatibility
Description: The spacecraft shall be mechanically and electrically compatible with Falcon 9 and Vulcan Centaur payload interfaces, including ESPA Grande ring adapter.

Name: Onboard Data Storage
Description: The mass memory unit shall provide a minimum of 256 Gbit of non-volatile storage with radiation-tolerant error detection and correction (EDAC), supporting a minimum write endurance of 100,000 cycles.

Name: End-of-Life Passivation
Description: Upon completion of the disposal maneuver, the spacecraft shall passivate all energy sources including batteries, propellant tanks, and reaction wheels within 30 days.

Name: Thermal Vacuum Test Requirements
Description: The spacecraft shall undergo thermal vacuum testing per MIL-STD-1540E with four cycles between -30°C and +60°C at qualification-level margins.

Name: Command Authentication
Description: All ground-to-spacecraft telecommands shall be authenticated using AES-256 encryption and SHA-256 message authentication codes to prevent unauthorized commanding.

Name: Deployment Mechanism Reliability
Description: The solar array and antenna deployment mechanisms shall demonstrate a reliability of 0.9995 or better for single-shot deployment, verified by a minimum of 3x life-cycle testing.

Name: Electromagnetic Shielding
Description: The avionics enclosure shall provide a minimum of 40 dB electromagnetic shielding effectiveness across 100 MHz to 10 GHz to protect sensitive electronics from external RF interference.

Name: Spacecraft Thermal Model Correlation
Description: The thermal mathematical model shall be correlated to thermal vacuum test data within ±3°C at all critical temperature monitoring points before flight prediction runs.
